lines.speaker: Hirko
lines.text: Hello there!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, what's in those boxes?

lines.speaker: Hirko
lines.text: Aha, interested in my crossbows are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are they any good?

lines.speaker: Hirko
lines.text: They're dwarven engineering at its best. If you've got enough skill in crafting, smithing and fletching, you can even make them yourself. You can buy some of the parts here and make the rest yourself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I make one for myself?

lines.speaker: Hirko
lines.text: Well, firstly you'll need to chop yourself some wood, then use a knife on the wood to whittle out a nice crossbow stock like these here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wood fletched into stock... check.

lines.speaker: Hirko
lines.text: Then get yourself some metal and a hammer and smith yourself some limbs for the bow, mind that you use the right metals and woods though as some wood is too light to use with some metal and vice versa.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Which goes with which?

lines.speaker: Hirko
lines.text: Wood and Bronze as they're basic materials, Oak and Blurite, Willow and Iron, Steel and Teak, Mithril and Maple, Adamantite and Mahogany and finally Runite and Yew.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, so I have my stock and a pair of limbs... what now?

lines.speaker: Hirko
lines.text: Simply take a hammer and smack the limbs firmly onto the stock. You'll then need a string, only they're not the same as normal bows. You'll need to dry some large animal's meat to get sinew, then spin that on a spinning

lines.speaker: Hirko
lines.text: wheel, it's the only thing we've found to be strong enough for a crossbow.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What about magic logs?

lines.speaker: Hirko
lines.text: Well.. I don't rightly know... us dwarves don't work with magic, we prefer gold and rock. Much more stable. I guess you could ask the humans at the rangers guild to see if they can do something but I don't want

lines.speaker: Hirko
lines.text: anything to do with it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for telling me. Bye!

lines.speaker: Hirko
lines.text: Take care, straight shooting.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What about ammo?

lines.speaker: Hirko
lines.text: You can smith yourself lots of different bolts, don't forget to flight them with feathers like you do arrows though. You can poison any untipped bolt but there's also the option of tipping them with gems then

lines.speaker: Hirko
lines.text: enchanting them with runes. This can have some pretty powerful effects.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh my poor bank, how will I store all those?!

lines.speaker: Hirko
lines.text: Don't you worry, I have just the thing here, a bolt pouch designed specially for holding 4 different types of crossbow bolt.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow, how much?

lines.speaker: Hirko
lines.text: Hmm... didn't I see you down here buying one the other day? You can only have one at a time I'm afraid.

lines.speaker: Hirko
lines.text: Well let's see now... for you? 1500 gold pieces should do the trick.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes I'll pay the 1500gp.

lines.speaker: Hirko
lines.text: Hand it over then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I don't have that much on me.

lines.speaker: Hirko
lines.text: Best go make some money then, shouldn't take you too long.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for telling me. Bye!

lines.speaker: Hirko
lines.text: Take care, straight shooting.

sections.title: Catching the player trying to steal from his crossbow stall

lines.speaker: Hirko
lines.text: Hey! Get your hands off there!

sections.title: Interacting with him after stealing from his crossbow stall recently

lines.speaker: Hirko
lines.text: Halt, thief!

lines.speaker: Hirko
lines.text: Guards, catch that thief!
